unresolved_validation_requirements.requirement: Run npm start (or ng serve) to verify the application runs and serves index.html
Open served page and confirm AppComponent displays weather data from WeatherService

metadata.requirement_excerpt: Continue implementation of the city-weather-app Angular application by scaffolding the Angular bootstrap files (index.html, main.ts, AppModule, AppComponent), wiring the existing WeatherService, updating package.json scripts to provide an entrypoint, and validating that the application compiles and ... [truncated 480 chars]

affected_files.rationale: HTML entrypoint that bootstraps the Angular application root selector
Root Angular module that declares AppComponent and provides WeatherService
Root component that injects WeatherService and binds weather data to the template
Template to display weather data consumed from WeatherService
Add npm scripts for "start", "build", and "test" to serve, compile, and test the Angular application

metadata.diff_preview:
--- +++ @@ -0,0 +1,13 @@+<!doctype html>
+<html lang="en">
+<head>
+  <meta charset="utf-8">
+  <title>CityWeatherApp</title>
+  <base href="/">
+  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
+  <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="favicon.ico">
+</head>
+<body>
+  <app-root></app-root>
+</body>
+</html>

--- +++ @@ -0,0 +1,80 @@+
+
+

City Weather App

+

Stay tuned to the cities you care about

+

+ The latest conditions are pulled straight from the WeatherService so you always know if you need an umbrella or sunscreen. +

+
+ +
+ +
+
